name: PostgreSQL Observability
author: OpsDB Platform
description: Monitor PostgreSQL 17 databases: availability, sessions, blocking, replication, WAL, vacuum, indexes, security, query performance, EXPLAIN plan analysis, alert event tracking, and metric drill-down panels.

pg17_availability.description: Server version number, standby status (0=primary,1=standby), and uptime in seconds.
pg17_availability.query: SELECT current_setting('server_version_num')::int AS server_version_num, pg_is_in_recovery()::int AS is_standby, EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (now() - pg_postmaster_start_time()))::numeric AS uptime_seconds

pg17_activity_sessions.description: Session breakdown: total, active, idle, idle-in-transaction, waiting; connection usage percentage versus max_connections.
pg17_activity_sessions.query: WITH s AS (SELECT setting::int AS max_connections FROM pg_settings WHERE name = 'max_connections') SELECT count(*) AS total_sessions, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'active') AS active_sessions, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle') AS idle_sessions, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle in transaction') AS idle_in_transaction_sessions, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle in transaction (aborted)') AS idle_in_transaction_aborted_sessions, count(*) FILTER (WHERE wait_event IS NOT NULL) AS waiting_sessions, s.max_connections, round((count(*)::numeric / NULLIF(s.max_connections, 0)) * 100, 2) AS connection_usage_pct FROM pg_stat_activity a CROSS JOIN s WHERE a.pid <> pg_backend_pid() GROUP BY s.max_connections

pg17_session_details.description: Aggregate risk metrics for active and risky sessions: longest active query (seconds), longest idle-in-transaction (seconds), longest wait (seconds).
pg17_session_details.query: SELECT count(*) AS risky_session_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'active') AS long_active_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - query_start) FILTER (WHERE state = 'active')), 0)::numeric AS max_active_query_seconds, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - xact_start) FILTER (WHERE state LIKE 'idle in transaction%')), 0)::numeric AS max_idle_xact_seconds, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - query_start) FILTER (WHERE wait_event IS NOT NULL)), 0)::numeric AS max_wait_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE pid <> pg_backend_pid() AND (state = 'active' OR state LIKE 'idle in transaction%' OR wait_event IS NOT NULL)

pg17_blocking_locks.description: Count of blocked backend processes and the worst blocked duration in seconds.
pg17_blocking_locks.query: SELECT count(*) AS blocking_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - blocked.query_start))::numeric, 0) AS max_blocked_duration_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity blocked JOIN pg_locks blocked_locks ON blocked_locks.pid = blocked.pid WHERE NOT blocked_locks.granted

pg17_long_transactions.description: Count of transactions open longer than 15 minutes and the age of the oldest one in seconds.
pg17_long_transactions.query: SELECT count(*) AS long_transaction_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - xact_start))::numeric, 0) AS max_transaction_age_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE xact_start IS NOT NULL AND now() - xact_start > interval '15 minutes' AND pid <> pg_backend_pid()

pg17_database_stats.description: Buffer cache hit percentage, temp file usage, deadlock count, commit/rollback counts for the current database.
pg17_database_stats.query: SELECT blks_read, blks_hit, round(blks_hit::numeric / NULLIF(blks_hit + blks_read, 0) * 100, 2) AS cache_hit_pct, temp_files, temp_bytes, deadlocks, xact_commit, xact_rollback FROM pg_stat_database WHERE datname = current_database()

pg17_io_stats.description: Aggregated I/O read/write counts, timing in ms, evictions, and buffer hits across all I/O backends (requires track_io_timing = on).
pg17_io_stats.query: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(reads), 0) AS total_reads, COALESCE(SUM(writes), 0) AS total_writes, COALESCE(SUM(read_time), 0) AS total_read_time_ms, COALESCE(SUM(write_time), 0) AS total_write_time_ms, COALESCE(SUM(evictions), 0) AS total_evictions, COALESCE(SUM(hits), 0) AS total_hits FROM pg_stat_io

pg17_wal_stats.description: WAL record count, full-page images, bytes generated, and WAL buffer full count from pg_stat_wal (PostgreSQL 17+).
pg17_wal_stats.query: SELECT wal_records, wal_fpi, wal_bytes, wal_buffers_full FROM pg_stat_wal

pg17_checkpoint_bgwriter.description: Checkpoint counts (timed vs requested), write/sync timing, buffer stats, and percentage of demand-triggered checkpoints. Uses pg_stat_checkpointer + pg_stat_bgwriter (PostgreSQL 17+).
pg17_checkpoint_bgwriter.query: SELECT c.num_timed AS checkpoints_timed, c.num_requested AS checkpoints_requested, c.write_time AS checkpoint_write_time_ms, c.sync_time AS checkpoint_sync_time_ms, c.buffers_written AS buffers_checkpoint, b.buffers_clean, b.maxwritten_clean, b.buffers_alloc, round(c.num_requested::numeric / NULLIF(c.num_timed + c.num_requested, 0) * 100, 2) AS requested_checkpoint_pct FROM pg_stat_checkpointer c, pg_stat_bgwriter b

pg17_physical_replication.description: Count of standbys, streaming standbys, maximum replay lag in bytes and seconds.
pg17_physical_replication.query: SELECT count(*) AS replication_standby_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'streaming') AS replication_streaming_count, COALESCE(max(pg_wal_lsn_diff(sent_lsn, replay_lsn)), 0) AS max_replay_lag_bytes, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(replay_lag))::numeric, 0) AS max_replay_lag_seconds FROM pg_stat_replication

pg17_wal_receiver.description: WAL receiver count and streaming receiver count (standby-side view).
pg17_wal_receiver.query: SELECT count(*) AS wal_receiver_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE status = 'streaming') AS wal_receiver_streaming_count FROM pg_stat_wal_receiver

pg17_replication_slots.description: Total, active, and inactive replication slot counts; maximum retained WAL bytes across all slots.
pg17_replication_slots.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_slots, count(*) FILTER (WHERE active) AS active_slots, count(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT active) AS inactive_slots, COALESCE(max(pg_wal_lsn_diff(pg_current_wal_lsn(), restart_lsn)), 0) AS max_retained_wal_bytes FROM pg_replication_slots

pg17_subscription_status.description: Logical replication subscription count, running subscriptions, and maximum receive lag in bytes.
pg17_subscription_status.query: SELECT count(*) AS subscription_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE pid IS NOT NULL) AS running_subscriptions, COALESCE(max(pg_wal_lsn_diff(latest_end_lsn, received_lsn)), 0) AS max_receive_lag_bytes FROM pg_stat_subscription

pg17_subscription_table_sync.description: Total subscribed tables, tables in ready state, and tables still synchronising.
pg17_subscription_table_sync.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_tables, count(*) FILTER (WHERE srsubstate = 'r') AS ready_tables, count(*) FILTER (WHERE srsubstate <> 'r') AS syncing_tables FROM pg_subscription_rel

pg17_table_vacuum_pressure.description: Tables with dead tuples, count with dead tuple ratio >= 20%, maximum dead tuple percentage, and total dead tuples.
pg17_table_vacuum_pressure.query: SELECT count(*) AS tables_with_dead_tuples, count(*) FILTER (WHERE round(n_dead_tup::numeric / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0) * 100, 2) >= 20) AS tables_high_dead_pct, COALESCE(max(round(n_dead_tup::numeric / NULLIF(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup, 0) * 100, 2)), 0) AS max_dead_tuple_pct, COALESCE(sum(n_dead_tup), 0) AS total_dead_tuples FROM pg_stat_user_tables WHERE n_live_tup + n_dead_tup > 0

pg17_vacuum_progress.description: Count of actively running VACUUM operations and the maximum heap scan progress percentage.
pg17_vacuum_progress.query: SELECT count(*) AS active_vacuum_count, COALESCE(max(round(heap_blks_scanned::numeric / NULLIF(heap_blks_total, 0) * 100, 2)), 0) AS max_vacuum_progress_pct FROM pg_stat_progress_vacuum

pg17_analyze_progress.description: Count of actively running ANALYZE operations.
pg17_analyze_progress.query: SELECT count(*) AS active_analyze_count FROM pg_stat_progress_analyze

pg17_index_usage.description: Total user indexes, count of unused (zero-scan) indexes, and total wasted space in unused indexes in bytes.
pg17_index_usage.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_user_indexes, count(*) FILTER (WHERE idx_scan = 0) AS unused_indexes, COALESCE(sum(CASE WHEN idx_scan = 0 THEN pg_relation_size(indexrelid) ELSE 0 END), 0) AS unused_index_size_bytes FROM pg_stat_user_indexes

pg17_table_sizes.description: Total user table count, combined size in bytes, and size of the largest single table in bytes.
pg17_table_sizes.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_tables, COALESCE(sum(pg_total_relation_size(c.oid)), 0) AS total_size_bytes, COALESCE(max(pg_total_relation_size(c.oid)), 0) AS largest_table_bytes FROM pg_class c JOIN pg_namespace n ON n.oid = c.relnamespace WHERE c.relkind IN ('r', 'p', 'm') AND n.nspname NOT IN ('pg_catalog', 'information_schema')

pg17_database_sizes.description: Current database total size in bytes.

pg17_top_queries_pg_stat_statements.description: Tracked query count, maximum mean execution time in ms, and total temp block spill in MB (requires pg_stat_statements extension).
pg17_top_queries_pg_stat_statements.query: SELECT substring(query, 1, 500) AS query, calls::int AS calls, round(mean_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS mean_exec_time_ms, round(total_exec_time::numeric, 2) AS total_exec_time_ms, rows::int AS total_rows FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 25

pg17_query_temp_spills.description: Count of queries with any temp block usage and the maximum temp spill for a single query in MB (requires pg_stat_statements).
pg17_query_temp_spills.query: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE temp_blks_read + temp_blks_written > 0) AS queries_with_temp_spill, COALESCE(max((temp_blks_read + temp_blks_written) * 8.0 / 1024), 0) AS max_temp_spill_mb FROM pg_stat_statements

pg17_configuration_baseline.description: 27 key GUC parameters: memory sizing, WAL durability, autovacuum, timeouts, security, and observability settings. Includes pending-restart count.
pg17_configuration_baseline.query: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE pending_restart) AS settings_pending_restart, max(CASE WHEN name='track_counts' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS track_counts, max(CASE WHEN name='track_io_timing' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS track_io_timing, max(CASE WHEN name='autovacuum' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS autovacuum, max(CASE WHEN name='log_checkpoints' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS log_checkpoints, max(CASE WHEN name='log_lock_waits' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS log_lock_waits, max(CASE WHEN name='log_connections' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS log_connections, max(CASE WHEN name='ssl' THEN (setting='on')::int END) AS ssl_enabled, max(CASE WHEN name='synchronous_commit' THEN (setting IN ('on','remote_apply','remote_write'))::int END) AS synchronous_commit_safe, max(CASE WHEN name='max_connections' THEN setting::numeric END) AS max_connections, max(CASE WHEN name='max_wal_senders' THEN setting::numeric END) AS max_wal_senders, max(CASE WHEN name='autovacuum_max_workers' THEN setting::numeric END) AS autovacuum_max_workers, max(CASE WHEN name='max_parallel_workers_per_gather' THEN setting::numeric END) AS max_parallel_workers, max(CASE WHEN name='checkpoint_completion_target' THEN setting::numeric END) AS checkpoint_completion_target, max(CASE WHEN name='random_page_cost' THEN setting::numeric END) AS random_page_cost, max(CASE WHEN name='autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor' THEN setting::numeric END) AS autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor, max(CASE WHEN name='log_min_duration_statement' THEN setting::numeric END) AS log_min_duration_statement, max(CASE WHEN name='idle_in_transaction_session_timeout' THEN setting::numeric END) AS idle_in_transaction_session_timeout_ms, max(CASE WHEN name='statement_timeout' THEN setting::numeric END) AS statement_timeout_ms, max(CASE WHEN name='lock_timeout' THEN setting::numeric END) AS lock_timeout_ms, max(CASE WHEN name='shared_buffers' THEN setting::numeric * 8192 END) AS shared_buffers_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='work_mem' THEN setting::numeric * 1024 END) AS work_mem_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='maintenance_work_mem' THEN setting::numeric * 1024 END) AS maintenance_work_mem_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='max_wal_size' THEN setting::numeric * 1048576 END) AS max_wal_size_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='effective_cache_size' THEN setting::numeric * 8192 END) AS effective_cache_size_bytes, max(CASE WHEN name='shared_preload_libraries' THEN (setting LIKE '%pg_stat_statements%')::int END) AS shared_preload_has_pss, max(CASE WHEN name='wal_level' THEN (setting IN ('replica','logical'))::int END) AS wal_level_ok FROM pg_settings WHERE name IN ('track_counts','track_io_timing','autovacuum','log_checkpoints','log_lock_waits','log_connections','ssl','synchronous_commit','max_connections','max_wal_senders','autovacuum_max_workers','max_parallel_workers_per_gather','checkpoint_completion_target','random_page_cost','autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor','log_min_duration_statement','idle_in_transaction_session_timeout','statement_timeout','lock_timeout','shared_buffers','work_mem','maintenance_work_mem','max_wal_size','effective_cache_size','shared_preload_libraries','wal_level')

pg17_extension_inventory.description: Total number of installed PostgreSQL extensions.
pg17_extension_inventory.query: SELECT count(*) AS installed_extensions_count FROM pg_extension

pg17_role_security.description: Total roles, superuser count, replication-only role count, and expired login role count.
pg17_role_security.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_roles, count(*) FILTER (WHERE rolsuper) AS superuser_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE rolreplication AND NOT rolsuper) AS replication_only_role_count, count(*) FILTER (WHERE rolcanlogin AND rolvaliduntil < now()) AS expired_login_role_count FROM pg_roles

pg17_database_privileges.description: Total database count and number of non-template user databases.
pg17_database_privileges.query: SELECT count(*) AS total_databases, count(*) FILTER (WHERE NOT datistemplate) AS user_databases_count FROM pg_database

pg17_autovacuum_activity.description: Count of active autovacuum worker processes and age of the longest-running autovacuum in seconds.
pg17_autovacuum_activity.query: SELECT count(*) AS active_autovacuum_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - query_start))::numeric, 0) AS max_autovacuum_age_seconds FROM pg_stat_activity WHERE (backend_type ILIKE '%autovacuum%' OR query ILIKE 'autovacuum:%') AND state IS NOT NULL

pg17_prepared_transactions.description: Count of prepared (two-phase commit) transactions and age of the oldest one in seconds.
pg17_prepared_transactions.query: SELECT count(*) AS prepared_transaction_count, COALESCE(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM max(now() - prepared))::numeric, 0) AS max_prepared_age_seconds FROM pg_prepared_xacts

pg17_invalid_indexes.description: Count of invalid or not-ready indexes and their total wasted disk space in bytes.
pg17_invalid_indexes.query: SELECT count(*) AS invalid_index_count, COALESCE(sum(pg_relation_size(c.oid)), 0) AS invalid_index_total_bytes FROM pg_index i JOIN pg_class c ON c.oid = i.indexrelid WHERE NOT i.indisvalid OR NOT i.indisready

pg17_partition_overview.description: Count of partitioned tables and the maximum number of partitions on a single table.
pg17_partition_overview.query: SELECT count(*) AS partitioned_table_count, COALESCE(max(child_count), 0) AS max_partition_count FROM (SELECT c.oid, count(child.inhrelid) AS child_count FROM pg_class c LEFT JOIN pg_inherits child ON child.inhparent = c.oid WHERE c.relkind = 'p' GROUP BY c.oid) t

pg17_xid_wraparound.description: XID age per database — exceeding 2.1 billion causes PostgreSQL to forcibly shut down to prevent wraparound data corruption. This is the #1 cause of unplanned PG outages.
pg17_xid_wraparound.query: SELECT max(age(datfrozenxid)) AS max_xid_age, count(*) FILTER (WHERE age(datfrozenxid) > 1500000000) AS databases_near_wraparound, count(*) FILTER (WHERE age(datfrozenxid) > 200000000) AS databases_needing_freeze, COALESCE(min(2147483647 - age(datfrozenxid)), 0) AS min_xids_until_wraparound FROM pg_database WHERE datallowconn

pg17_table_activity.description: Table-level DML throughput and scan patterns: total inserts/updates/deletes, sequential vs index scan ratio, HOT update percentage, and large tables being sequentially scanned.
pg17_table_activity.query: SELECT COALESCE(sum(seq_scan), 0) AS total_seq_scans, COALESCE(sum(idx_scan), 0) AS total_idx_scans, COALESCE(round(sum(seq_scan)::numeric / NULLIF(sum(seq_scan) + sum(idx_scan), 0) * 100, 2), 0) AS seq_scan_pct, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_ins), 0) AS total_inserts, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_upd), 0) AS total_updates, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_del), 0) AS total_deletes, COALESCE(sum(n_tup_hot_upd), 0) AS total_hot_updates, COALESCE(round(sum(n_tup_hot_upd)::numeric / NULLIF(sum(n_tup_upd), 0) * 100, 2), 0) AS hot_update_pct, count(*) FILTER (WHERE seq_scan > 100 AND n_live_tup > 10000) AS large_tables_seqscanned FROM pg_stat_user_tables

postgres.activity_locks.description: [Legacy v1.0] Active sessions, waiting sessions, idle-in-transaction, and blocked lock counts.
postgres.activity_locks.query: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'active') AS active_sessions, count(*) FILTER (WHERE wait_event_type IS NOT NULL) AS waiting_sessions, count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle in transaction') AS idle_in_transaction_sessions, (SELECT count(*) FROM pg_locks WHERE NOT granted) AS blocked_locks FROM pg_stat_activity

postgres.global_stats.description: [Legacy v1.0] Cache hit ratio, transaction counts, deadlocks, temp usage, database size, and XID age.
postgres.global_stats.query: SELECT coalesce(d.numbackends, 0) AS active_sessions, coalesce(d.xact_commit + d.xact_rollback, 0) AS total_transactions, CASE WHEN coalesce(d.blks_hit + d.blks_read, 0) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE round((d.blks_hit::numeric * 100.0) / nullif(d.blks_hit + d.blks_read, 0), 2) END AS cache_hit_percent, coalesce(d.deadlocks, 0) AS deadlocks, coalesce(d.temp_files, 0) AS temp_files, coalesce(d.temp_bytes, 0) AS temp_bytes, pg_database_size(current_database()) AS current_database_size_bytes, age(pg.datfrozenxid) AS max_freeze_age FROM pg_stat_database d JOIN pg_database pg ON pg.datname = d.datname WHERE d.datname = current_database()

postgres.storage_vacuum.description: [Legacy v1.0] Live/dead tuple counts, dead tuple percentage, and vacuum/autovacuum run counts.
postgres.storage_vacuum.query: SELECT coalesce(sum(n_live_tup), 0) AS live_tuples, coalesce(sum(n_dead_tup), 0) AS dead_tuples, CASE WHEN coalesce(sum(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup), 0) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE round((sum(n_dead_tup)::numeric * 100.0) / nullif(sum(n_live_tup + n_dead_tup), 0), 2) END AS dead_tuple_percent, coalesce(sum(vacuum_count), 0) AS vacuum_count, coalesce(sum(autovacuum_count), 0) AS autovacuum_count FROM pg_stat_user_tables

pg17_connection_usage_warning.title: Connection Usage High (Warning)
pg17_connection_usage_warning.recommendation: Review active and idle connections. Consider connection pooling (PgBouncer) and tuning max_connections.

pg17_connection_usage_critical.title: Connection Usage Critical
pg17_connection_usage_critical.recommendation: Immediate action required: nearly all connections are consumed. Terminate idle connections or enable connection pooling.

pg17_idle_in_transaction_warning.title: Idle-in-Transaction Sessions Detected
pg17_idle_in_transaction_warning.recommendation: Sessions holding open transactions block vacuuming and lock resources. Set idle_in_transaction_session_timeout in postgresql.conf.

pg17_idle_in_transaction_critical.title: High Idle-in-Transaction Session Count
pg17_idle_in_transaction_critical.recommendation: Many sessions are stuck in open transactions. Investigate application connection management and terminate stale backends.

pg17_waiting_sessions_detected.title: Waiting Sessions Detected
pg17_waiting_sessions_detected.recommendation: Multiple sessions are waiting on events or locks. Investigate pg_stat_activity for wait_event details.

pg17_long_active_query_warning.title: Long-Running Active Query (15+ min)
pg17_long_active_query_warning.recommendation: A query has been running for over 15 minutes. Review pg_stat_activity and consider using statement_timeout.

pg17_long_active_query_critical.title: Long-Running Active Query (1+ hour)
pg17_long_active_query_critical.recommendation: A query has been running for over 1 hour. Investigate and consider terminating via pg_terminate_backend().

pg17_blocking_lock_detected.title: Blocking Lock Detected
pg17_blocking_lock_detected.recommendation: At least one backend is blocked waiting for a lock. Identify blocking PIDs in pg_blocking_pids() and evaluate if they should be terminated.

pg17_blocking_lock_prolonged.title: Prolonged Blocking Lock (15+ min)
pg17_blocking_lock_prolonged.recommendation: A backend has been blocked for over 15 minutes. Consider terminating the blocking transaction to restore throughput.

pg17_long_transaction_warning.title: Long Open Transaction (15+ min)
pg17_long_transaction_warning.recommendation: A transaction has been open for over 15 minutes. Long transactions prevent autovacuum from reclaiming dead tuples.

pg17_long_transaction_critical.title: Long Open Transaction (1+ hour)
pg17_long_transaction_critical.recommendation: A transaction has been open for over 1 hour. This can cause table bloat and XID wraparound issues. Investigate and terminate if stale.

pg17_cache_hit_low_warning.title: Cache Hit Rate Below 95%
pg17_cache_hit_low_warning.recommendation: Buffer cache hit rate is low. Consider increasing shared_buffers or reviewing query access patterns.

pg17_cache_hit_low_critical.title: Cache Hit Rate Below 90%
pg17_cache_hit_low_critical.recommendation: Critical: most reads are going to disk. Urgently review shared_buffers, indexes, and query plans.

pg17_deadlocks_detected.title: Deadlocks Detected
pg17_deadlocks_detected.recommendation: Deadlocks indicate conflicting lock acquisition order. Review application transaction patterns and use consistent lock ordering.

pg17_replication_lag_bytes_warning.title: Replication Replay Lag High (1 GB+)
pg17_replication_lag_bytes_warning.recommendation: Standby is more than 1 GB behind the primary. Check network throughput, standby CPU, and replication slot health.

pg17_replication_lag_bytes_critical.title: Replication Replay Lag Critical (5 GB+)
pg17_replication_lag_bytes_critical.recommendation: Standby is more than 5 GB behind. Failover RPO is at serious risk. Investigate immediately.

pg17_replication_lag_seconds_warning.title: Replication Apply Lag High (30+ seconds)
pg17_replication_lag_seconds_warning.recommendation: Standby apply lag exceeds 30 seconds. Review wal_receiver_status_interval and standby workload.

pg17_replication_lag_seconds_critical.title: Replication Apply Lag Critical (5+ minutes)
pg17_replication_lag_seconds_critical.recommendation: Standby apply lag exceeds 5 minutes. DR capability is severely degraded. Investigate standby apply throughput immediately.

pg17_inactive_replication_slot.title: Inactive Replication Slot
pg17_inactive_replication_slot.recommendation: Inactive replication slots prevent WAL cleanup and can cause disk full conditions. Drop unused slots immediately.

pg17_retained_wal_warning.title: Replication Slot Retaining Large WAL (5 GB+)
pg17_retained_wal_warning.recommendation: A replication slot is holding back WAL cleanup. Monitor pg_replication_slots.restart_lsn and consider dropping inactive slots.

pg17_retained_wal_critical.title: Replication Slot Retaining Excessive WAL (10 GB+)
pg17_retained_wal_critical.recommendation: A replication slot is retaining 10 GB+ of WAL. Disk exhaustion risk is high. Drop inactive slots or reduce wal_keep_size.

pg17_checkpoint_ratio_warning.title: Requested Checkpoint Ratio High (20%+)
pg17_checkpoint_ratio_warning.recommendation: More than 20% of checkpoints are demand-triggered. Increase max_wal_size or checkpoint_completion_target.

pg17_checkpoint_ratio_critical.title: Requested Checkpoint Ratio Critical (50%+)
pg17_checkpoint_ratio_critical.recommendation: Over half of all checkpoints are demand-triggered. Significantly increase max_wal_size to reduce write pressure.

pg17_dead_tuple_pct_warning.title: High Dead Tuple Percentage (20%+)
pg17_dead_tuple_pct_warning.recommendation: At least one table has over 20% dead tuples. Check autovacuum settings and long-running transactions.

pg17_dead_tuple_pct_critical.title: Critical Dead Tuple Percentage (40%+)
pg17_dead_tuple_pct_critical.recommendation: A table has over 40% dead tuples causing significant bloat and degraded performance. Run VACUUM ANALYZE immediately.

pg17_old_prepared_transaction_warning.title: Old Prepared Transaction (15+ min)
pg17_old_prepared_transaction_warning.recommendation: A two-phase commit transaction has been prepared for over 15 minutes. This prevents vacuum from cleaning dead tuples.

pg17_old_prepared_transaction_critical.title: Old Prepared Transaction (1+ hour)
pg17_old_prepared_transaction_critical.recommendation: A prepared transaction is over 1 hour old. This can block XID wraparound protection. Roll back or commit via ROLLBACK/COMMIT PREPARED.

pg17_invalid_index_found.title: Invalid Index Detected
pg17_invalid_index_found.recommendation: One or more indexes are invalid (CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY failure or pg_upgrade). Rebuild them with REINDEX CONCURRENTLY.

pg17_superuser_count_high.title: Superuser Account Count High
pg17_superuser_count_high.recommendation: Minimise superuser accounts. Grant only the specific privileges needed. Review role membership in pg_roles.

pg17_settings_pending_restart.title: Configuration Changes Pending Restart
pg17_settings_pending_restart.recommendation: One or more postgresql.conf changes require a server restart to take effect. Plan a maintenance window.

pg17_xid_wraparound_warning.title: XID Wraparound Risk — Age Exceeds 1.5 Billion
pg17_xid_wraparound_warning.recommendation: One or more databases are approaching XID wraparound (limit: ~2.1B). Run VACUUM FREEZE on affected databases immediately and check autovacuum_freeze_max_age. Plan a maintenance VACUUM FREEZE if age > 1.9B.

pg17_xid_wraparound_critical.title: XID Wraparound Imminent — Age Exceeds 2 Billion
pg17_xid_wraparound_critical.recommendation: EMERGENCY: PostgreSQL will force-shutdown at ~2.1B XIDs to prevent data corruption. Run VACUUM FREEZE immediately. If the database is too large, consider pg_upgrade or coordinated freeze. Do not restart without running VACUUM FREEZE first.

pg17_ssl_disabled.title: SSL/TLS Not Enabled
pg17_ssl_disabled.recommendation: Enable SSL by setting ssl = on in postgresql.conf and providing ssl_cert_file / ssl_key_file. All connections should be encrypted in transit. Enforce via pg_hba.conf using hostssl entries.

pg17_no_idle_txn_timeout.title: idle_in_transaction_session_timeout Not Set
pg17_no_idle_txn_timeout.recommendation: Set idle_in_transaction_session_timeout = '60s' to automatically terminate sessions that hold open transactions without activity. Without this, a single stuck client can block autovacuum and cause lock pileups indefinitely.

pg17_synchronous_commit_off.title: synchronous_commit Disabled — Data Loss Risk
pg17_synchronous_commit_off.recommendation: synchronous_commit = off trades durability for throughput. A server crash within ~200ms can lose committed transactions. Set to 'on' for OLTP workloads. If you need the performance, use it only for specific non-critical tables via SET LOCAL.

pg17_high_seq_scan_ratio.title: High Sequential Scan Ratio (50%+)
pg17_high_seq_scan_ratio.recommendation: More than half of all table scans are sequential. Review pg_stat_user_tables for large tables with high seq_scan counts. Add indexes for frequent query predicates. Check random_page_cost vs effective_cache_size if plans look wrong.

cache_hit_ratio_low.title: [Legacy] Cache Hit Ratio Low
cache_hit_ratio_low.recommendation: Review shared buffers, query patterns, and index usage for excessive disk reads.

dead_tuples_high.title: [Legacy] Dead Tuples High
dead_tuples_high.recommendation: Review autovacuum health, long transactions, and tables with high dead tuple percentages.

replication_lag_high.title: [Legacy] Replication Lag High
replication_lag_high.recommendation: Check replica connectivity, WAL shipping, replication slots, and downstream apply delay.
